lesson.menu_desc: Say how you feel and describe symptoms -- I feel, I have a... -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 18 -- Talking About Health -- saying how you feel and describing symptoms
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Carolina Paludetto Rodrigues | Aula 18 -- Talking About Health

listenings.text: Hi Carolina, it's Bruno. I'm sorry, but I can't come to school today. I don't feel well at all. I woke up this morning and I had a terrible headache. I think I have a fever too, because I feel really hot and tired. My mom says I look pale, so she wants me to stay in bed and rest. I took some medicine after breakfast, but I still feel weak and I have no energy. I really wanted to play handball with you this afternoon, but I think I should stay home and get better. Can you tell the teacher I am sick? I hope I feel healthy again tomorrow. Thanks, my friend. Talk soon. Bye.

Good morning, everyone. This is the school nurse with a quick message about staying healthy this season. Many students feel tired and a few have a cold or a fever. So here are some simple tips. First, drink a lot of water every day, because water gives you energy. Second, eat fruit and vegetables, because healthy food helps your body fight a cold. Third, sleep at least eight hours, because sleep makes you feel strong. If you feel sick, with a headache or a fever, please tell a teacher and go home to rest. Don't share your water bottle, and always wash your hands. Remember, a healthy body helps a happy mind. Take care of yourselves, and have a great day.

extra_audio.text: Bruno is not feeling well today. First, he wakes up with a bad headache and feels very hot. Then, he tells Carolina he thinks he has a fever and no energy. After that, he takes some medicine and stays in bed to rest. Finally, he hopes he will feel healthy again tomorrow and asks Carolina to tell the teacher he is sick.
